run_metadata.notes: Threat model (scoped): This migration only alters FK delete behavior on existing public/auth references. Attacker with SQL DDL access could already change constraints; client-facing risk is unchanged. ON DELETE SET NULL on created_by weakens audit attribution when a user is deleted but does not grant new read paths or bypass RLS. No secrets or new endpoints introduced.

agent.name: security-and-privacy-auditor
agent.role: Identify practical security and privacy risks with realistic attack scenarios.

files_skipped: netlify/functions, auth middleware — out of scope

next_actions.action: Document retention policy if created_by NULL after user deletion is unacceptable for compliance
next_actions.rationale: SET NULL is a product/compliance choice, not an auth bypass; escalate only if audits require immutable actor attribution.
